# Artifact Contract Template

**The frontmatter schema every artifact-producing skill writes. `manifest-sync.ts` indexes from these fields. Downstream eval skills read them. Get the contract wrong → indexing breaks, eval skills can't find their inputs, the chain fractures.**

This file is the canonical schema. Skills cite it instead of re-explaining frontmatter requirements. When the schema needs to change, change it here first, propagate via Phase 2 ripple coordination.

---

## Required fields (every artifact)

| Field | Type | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `skill` | kebab-case string | `run-pipeline`, `write-copy`, `review-work` |
| `version` | integer (starts at 1) | `1`, `2` |
| `date` | ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` | `2026-05-16` |
| `status` | enum | `done` \| `done_with_concerns` \| `blocked` \| `needs_context` |

These four fields are non-negotiable for new artifacts. Two tools enforce them at different strictness, by design: `manifest-sync.ts` (the indexer) is **lenient** — it warns and falls back to inference (`skill` from path, `date` from mtime, `status` → `done`/`done_with_concerns`, `version` → 1) so a partial tree still indexes; `validate-artifacts.ts --strict` (the gate, live since Phase 0) **fails-fast** on any missing or invalid required field and is wired into the pre-merge gate. So a missing required field indexes as a degraded entry *and* blocks the merge — fix it at the producer skill.

The status enum maps to the [Completion Status Protocol](../CLAUDE.md#completion-status-protocol) — `done` means meets all requirements + passes critic gate; the others surface limitations explicitly rather than hiding them.

---

## Selection fields (every non-terminal artifact)

A "non-terminal" artifact is one another skill or future session will read. Selection fields tell consumers when to use it. Required when `lifecycle` ≠ `ephemeral`.

| Field | Type | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `summary` | one-line string | `"[loop] measurable improvement loop"` |
| `purpose` | one-line string | `"Operating program for a measurable strategy -> execution -> evaluation loop"` |
| `lifecycle` | enum (see below) | `loop`, `decision`, `snapshot`, `pipeline` |
| `use_when` | one-line string | `"Coordinating repeated strategy, execution, evaluation, and keep/discard decisions for this initiative"` |

`artifact-index.md` (the human-readable companion to manifest.json) is generated from these — write them as if a future agent is choosing between 30 artifacts and needs ~one line to decide.

---

## Lifecycle enum

**Canonical source: [`references/_shared/manifest-spec.md`](manifest-spec.md) § "Field semantics" — `lifecycle` line.** That file lists the full 15-value enum as `manifest-sync.ts` recognizes it. Skill authors should treat that line as authoritative.

Author-facing summary (the values you'll set in frontmatter):

| Lifecycle | Behavior | Typical producers |
|---|---|---|
| `canonical` | Edited in place, kept forever, human-curated | `create-brand` → `brand/`, `architect-system` → `architecture/`, `research-icp` → `research/`, this ref itself |
| `loop` | Operating contract for a measurable initiative | `run-pipeline` → `.forsvn/loops/{slug}/program.md` |
| `loop-context` | Local assumptions, baselines, source links | `run-pipeline` → `context.md` |
| `strategy` | Loop-specific plans, hypotheses, briefs | Strategy skills inside a loop folder |
| `execution` | Publish-ready marketing/content assets | Marketing/content execution skills inside a loop folder |
| `evaluation` | Post-execution metric snapshots, scoring | `*-eval` skills inside a loop folder |
| `learning` | Promoted evidence-backed lessons for future cycles | `run-pipeline` → `learnings.md` |
| `pipeline` | One-time skill output; regenerated by re-running | `write-copy`, `write-ad`, `write-outreach`, etc. → `docs/forsvn/artifacts/marketing/...` |
| `decision` | Dated immutable record of strategic choice | `debate-agents` → `docs/forsvn/artifacts/meta/decisions/[date]-<slug>.md` |
| `snapshot` | Dated point-in-time record | `review-work`, `clean-artifacts` → `docs/forsvn/artifacts/meta/records/[date]-<slug>.md` |
| `spec` | Working-draft specs awaiting promotion | `discover` → `docs/forsvn/artifacts/meta/specs/<slug>.md` |
| `ephemeral` | Working files; cleanup-artifacts default target | `docs/forsvn/artifacts/.archive/` after cleanup |

Derived/inferred values (set by `manifest-sync.ts` or `clean-artifacts`, NOT set by skill authors):

- `anchor` — auto-applied to session-anchor artifacts (e.g., `roadmap.md`, `tasks.md`)
- `registry` — auto-applied to living-registry files inside `meta/records/` (`kind: registry` frontmatter)
- `archive` — auto-applied to anything under `.archive/` post-cleanup

`sketch` is a working-draft folder convention (`meta/sketches/`) not yet a tracked lifecycle value — historically conflated with `spec`. Phase 2 task-breakdown / cleanup-artifacts refactors will decide whether to add it to the enum.

`agent-skills/CLAUDE.md § "Lifecycle taxonomy"` is a maintainer-facing summary that should mirror this table; if drift appears, this file (and manifest-spec.md) win because CLAUDE.md is not shipped to users.

---

## Optional / context-dependent fields

| Field | When to include | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `do_not_use_when` | Sharpens selection — when the artifact is wrong fit | `"The work has no observable metric or attribution path"` |
| `upstream` | Names what produced or fed this artifact | `"operator intent, prior artifacts, metric baseline"` |
| `downstream` | Names what consumes this artifact | `"strategy skills, marketing/content execution skills, evaluation skills"` |
| `loop` | When `lifecycle` is loop / strategy / execution / evaluation / learning | `"q3-launch-shortform"` |
| `provenance` | Two variants — see below | YAML block, schema depends on variant |
| `consumers` | When this artifact is read by multiple downstream skills | `"every creative marketing skill"` |
| `supersedes` | When this artifact replaces a prior one (by `id`) | `"old-plan-slug"` |
| `superseded_by` | Set on the old artifact when a newer one replaces it (by `id`) | `"new-plan-slug"` |
| `references` | Non-hierarchical link to a related artifact (by `id`) | `"market, icp"` |
| `kind` | When marking a living registry inside `records/` | `"registry"` |
| `assets` | The return-leg (§ below) — re-ingested rendered outputs attached to this artifact | `[.forsvn/assets/hero/final.png, https://site.com/lp]` |
| `asset_picked` | The option-picker's canonical choice among `assets` (or a variant slug) | `.forsvn/assets/hero/final.png` |
| `execution_mode` | The execution-fork choice recorded on a brief/produce artifact (provenance + eval attribution) | `brief-only` \| `assisted` \| `direct` |

**Graph edges are authored by `id`, not path** (Phase 1). The five edge fields (`upstream`, `downstream`, `supersedes`, `superseded_by`, `references`) reference other artifacts by their stable `id`; the manifest resolves `id → current path` and derives the `referenced_by` reverse index, so a move never breaks an edge. Tokens that name something outside the indexed graph (a skill name, an external path like `skills/…` or `../_biz-ops/…`) are kept literal and treated as external. See [`manifest-spec.md`](manifest-spec.md) § "v2 — the Knowledge Graph".

### `provenance:` — two variants

The `provenance:` block has two distinct uses. Pick the variant that matches what the artifact *is*, not what produced it.

**Variant A — Extraction provenance** (Gate 7 chain-hardening). Use when this artifact's content was extracted or refactored out of another source file. Schema:

```yaml
provenance:
  extracted_from: <source path or section>
  extracted_at: YYYY-MM-DD
```

Producers: refactor passes, ref-extraction commits. Consumer: chain-hardening audit (reconstructs lineage when source material moves).

**Variant B — Generation provenance** (eval-loop chain-hardening). Use when this artifact was generated by a skill and may be evaluated in the future. **Required** for artifacts produced inside `.forsvn/loops/<slug>/strategy/` or `.forsvn/loops/<slug>/execution/`, and for any `docs/forsvn/artifacts/` output a downstream `evaluate-*` skill is expected to consume. Schema:

```yaml
provenance:
  skill: <kebab-case-skill-name>
  run_date: YYYY-MM-DD
  input_artifacts:
    - <path>
    - <path>
  output_eval: <path or null until eval runs>
```

Producers: every generative skill in scope of an eval loop. Consumer: `evaluate-*` skills (read `input_artifacts` to ground scoring), promotion script (reads `output_eval` to walk the artifact → eval → learning chain), `manifest-sync` (indexes `skill` + `run_date`).

The eval-loop contract is the source of this variant. It is emitted by skills whose output is expected to be scored by a downstream `evaluate-*` command.

A single artifact MAY carry both variants under separate keys (`provenance_extraction:` and `provenance_generation:`) if it was extracted from a source AND is generation-evaluatable, but this is rare — typically extraction-provenance artifacts are canonical refs and aren't evaluated.

### Return-leg fields (`assets` / `asset_picked`)

The closed loop (CLOSED-LOOP.md §6) only closes when the **real rendered output**
re-enters the graph — so the evaluator scores what shipped and downstream skills
(`write-social`, `write-ad`) reference the asset, not the brief.

- **`assets`** — a **flat list** of repo-relative paths or URLs of rendered outputs
  attached to this artifact. Files are taken into custody under
  `.forsvn/assets/<id>/` (id-keyed so a move never orphans them) and the blob is
  git-ignored (local-first: track the pointer, not the binary); URLs are recorded
  verbatim. Populated by `forsvn-preview attach <artifact.md> <file-or-url>` (the
  desktop drop-zone is a thin trigger over the same mechanic).
- **`asset_picked`** — the **option-picker's** canonical choice among `assets`
  (or, for a design-variant picker, the chosen variant slug). Persisted from the
  review surface's variant picker (`--picked` on `attach`, or the picker POST).

Both are optional and flat-YAML; `manifest-sync` indexes them and `forsvn-mcp
get_artifact` surfaces them so an agent references the picked asset directly.

---

## Stack + provenance fields (every artifact, v2)

Three flat fields tell consumers which stack produced this and which surface
carries the review. Required on every artifact.

| Field | Type | Required | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `stack` | enum `meta \| research \| marketing \| product` | yes | `marketing` |
| `skills_involved` | list of kebab-case skill slugs | optional (multi-skill pipelines) | `[research-icp, create-brand]` |
| `review_surface` | enum `html \| md \| none` | yes | `html` |

`stack` is mandatory and **must equal the artifact's `<stack>/` folder name** —
zero translation between path and frontmatter. The legacy `mkt` value is retired:
use `marketing`. `skills_involved` enumerates every contributing skill in a
multi-skill pipeline; `skill:` (the primary producer) stays as before.
`review_surface` declares which surface this artifact uses (`html` = HTML preview
rendered by the forsvn-preview review module (within the single `forsvn` plugin)
while `decision_state: pending`; `md` = Markdown only; `none` =
`decision_state: not_required`).

## v3 — the by-stack layered home + instruction core (2026-05-28)

Artifacts no longer live in one flat dump. The `docs/forsvn/` home is the
project's human-readable knowledge layer, organized as three layers × by stack;
`.forsvn/` stays the machine-state/discovery marker:

```
docs/forsvn/canonical/<stack>/<UPPER-NAME>.md          ① TRUTH (curated, edited in place)
docs/forsvn/artifacts/<stack>/<skill>-<date>-<slug>.md ② KNOWLEDGE (working output)
docs/forsvn/experience/<stack>/<name>.md               ③ MEMORY (append-only learnings)
   stacks: meta · research · marketing · product   (folder name == `stack`)
```

The path grammar is checked by `lint-artifact-paths.ts`; canonical names are
UPPERCASE (`ARCHITECTURE`, `USER-FLOW`, `MASTER-PLAN`, `BRAND`, `DESIGN`,
`PHASE-LEDGER`, `ICP`, `MARKET`). The old flat
`docs/forsvn/artifacts/<stack>-<skill>-<date>-<slug>.md` grammar is **legacy** —
recognized for back-compat indexing + the un-flatten migration, never emitted.

### Three new required fields (the instruction core)

| Field | Type | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | kebab slug, immutable | yes | **stable identity** — the manifest maps `id → current path`, so moving / renaming an artifact never breaks a reference |
| `type` | enum (below) | yes | **per-doc instruction** — tells an agent how to use this document |
| `keywords` | non-empty list | yes | **greppable selection surface** — `rg "keywords:.*\bbrand\b"` finds it with no tooling |

`type` enum: `canonical · plan · spec · decision · experience · pipeline ·
snapshot · review · brief · strategy · execution · evaluation · loop · learning ·
registry`. (`lifecycle` stays as the manifest's sort axis; when only one of
`type`/`lifecycle` is set, the indexer derives the other.)

**Per-type self-instruction** — the instruction lives with the document:

- `type: canonical` → "I'm durable truth — load me before any work touching `<stack>`."
- `type: plan` / `spec` → "I'm a working spec — read me to resume `<initiative>`."
- `type: experience` → "I'm a learning — read me before doing `<thing>` so you don't re-ask / repeat a mistake."
- `type: decision` → "I'm a committed choice — honor me; don't relitigate."

### Frontmatter is tiered (a small instruction core, not an 18-field bag)

| Tier | Fields | Who reads it |
|---|---|---|
| **Instruction core** (greppable, per-type) | `id`, `type`, `stack`, `keywords`, `use_when`, `do_not_use_when` | **agents**, to decide what to load and where to look |
| **Bookkeeping** (tooling/UI) | `version`, `date`, `status`, `decision_state`, `review_surface`, `reviewed_at`, `reviewer` | manifest, validators, the preview module |
| **Graph** (edges, by `id`) | `upstream`, `downstream`, `supersedes`, `superseded_by`, `references` | UI connections + agent context traversal |

**Frontmatter is the source of truth; the manifest is a derived optimization.**
Even with no manifest, `rg` over flat keys answers "what is this, do I use it,
where do I look." `bun skills/bin/validate-artifacts.ts --strict` enforces the
**required** fields across all three layers — `skill`, `version`, `date`,
`status`, `stack`, `review_surface`, plus the v3 instruction-core `id`, `type`,
`keywords` (and internal graph edges authored by `id`, not path). `use_when` /
`do_not_use_when` sit in the instruction core *conceptually* — they are what an
agent reads to decide — but they are recommended selection context (§ Selection
fields), **not** strict-enforced; author them on every non-terminal artifact.

## Review fields (reviewable artifacts)

Artifacts whose lifecycle requires human sign-off carry four flat review fields.
Full semantics: [[reviewable-artifact-contract]].

| Field | Type | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `decision_state` | enum | `pending` \| `approved` \| `denied` \| `suggested` \| `not_required` |
| `review_tool` | enum | `proof` \| `inline` \| `roughdraft` \| `none` |
| `reviewed_at` | ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` or empty | `2026-05-22` |
| `reviewer` | string or empty | `operator` |

Fields are flat by design — `manifest-sync.ts` parses flat YAML only. Absent or
unrecognized `decision_state` normalizes to `not_required`, so legacy artifacts
index unchanged. `manifest-sync` indexes `decision_state` into a Decision column
in `artifact-index.md`.

### Collaborative-doc sub-type (`review_tool: proof`)

An opt-in sub-type for iterative, long-form artifacts reviewed in the **Proof**
editor instead of via the checkbox + CriticMarkup path (ADR
`docs/adr/2026-06-08-proof-collab-docs.md`). When `review_tool: proof`, three
additive flat-scalar fields bind the artifact to its Proof working doc:

| Field | Type | Example | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `proof_slug` | string | `mx6sy15o` | **Required** when `review_tool: proof` — the MCP proxy resolves the working doc by this. |
| `proof_doc_id` | string or empty | `5c7bdbfe-…` | Proof's stable doc UUID; informational. |
| `collab_state` | enum | `drafting` \| `in_review` \| `exported` | Lifecycle of the Proof-backed doc. |

Proof owns only *transient working state*; this `.md` stays canonical and is
updated on human accept via `forsvn-collab export` — written by the plugin's atomic
byte-fidelity writer on the CLI path and by `forsvn-core` on the desktop path (deferred
with editor embedding); one trusted writer per path. Agents post *suggestions* via the
MCP proxy and never write the
decision (§9.2=B). `validate-artifacts --strict` requires `proof_slug` whenever
`review_tool: proof`; `manifest-sync` drops an unknown `collab_state` with a warning.

**When to set `decision_state: pending`** — by lifecycle:

| Lifecycle | Default |
|---|---|
| `canonical`, `decision`, `spec` | `pending` — required before the artifact is final |
| `strategy`, `execution` | `pending` inside a loop, before publish/execute |
| `pipeline` | `not_required` (a skill may opt in) |
| `evaluation`, `learning`, `snapshot` | `not_required` |

Reviewable artifacts also carry a `## Review Gate` body block:

````markdown
## Review Gate
- [ ] Approve
- [ ] Deny
- [ ] Suggest changes

Comments and suggested edits use Proof or inline CriticMarkup, depending on `review_tool`.
````

The human checks exactly one box; the agent reads it to set `decision_state`:
Approve → `approved`, Deny → `denied`, Suggest changes → `suggested`.

CriticMarkup review notes live in the **body**, never in frontmatter. `status`
(skill quality gate) and `decision_state` (human acceptance) are independent —
`status: done` with `decision_state: pending` is valid and expected.

---

## Minimum viable example (terminal artifact)

A `review-work` report that won't be re-read:

```yaml
---
skill: review-work
version: 1
date: 2026-05-16
status: done
stack: meta
review_surface: none
id: fresh-eyes-<slug>
type: review
keywords: [review, fresh-eyes]
---

# Fresh-Eyes Review: <slug>

...
```

The four base required fields, the two v2 mandatories (`stack`, `review_surface`),
and the v3 instruction core (`id`, `type`, `keywords`). No other selection fields
because the artifact is one-shot (read once, archive). No `decision_state` because
terminal snapshots default to `not_required`.

---

## Full feature example (non-terminal, in a loop)

A `run-pipeline` (eval-only mode) `program.md` for an initiative loop:

```yaml
---
skill: run-pipeline
version: 1
date: 2026-05-16
status: done
stack: meta
review_surface: md
id: tiktok-hold-rate-loop
type: loop
keywords: [loop, tiktok, hold-rate]
summary: "[loop] tiktok hold-rate improvement loop"
purpose: "Operating program for measurable hold-rate strategy -> execution -> evaluation"
lifecycle: loop
loop: tiktok-hold-rate
use_when: "Coordinating repeated strategy, execution, evaluation, and keep/discard decisions for tiktok hold-rate"
do_not_use_when: "The work has no observable metric or attribution path"
upstream: "operator intent, prior cycle learnings, platform analytics baseline"
downstream: "short-form-brief, short-form-eval"
---
```

---

## Variation by lifecycle

**Decision artifact** (`debate-agents`):

```yaml
---
skill: debate-agents
version: 1
date: 2026-05-16
status: done
stack: meta
review_surface: md
id: conquis-pricing-59-single-tier
type: decision
keywords: [decision, pricing, conquis]
summary: "Decision: ship Conquis desktop $59 single-tier pricing"
purpose: "Operator-committed pricing decision after 3-perspective debate"
lifecycle: decision
use_when: "Reading the rationale behind the $59 single-tier choice"
upstream: "discover spec, market-research"
decision_state: approved
review_tool: inline
reviewed_at: 2026-05-16
reviewer: operator
---
```

**Snapshot artifact** (`review-work`):

```yaml
---
skill: review-work
version: 1
date: 2026-05-16
status: done_with_concerns
stack: meta
review_surface: none
id: eval-loop-refactor-fresh-eyes
type: review
keywords: [review, eval-loop, refactor]
summary: "Fresh-eyes on eval-loop refactor (FIXED 2 inline, 1 deferred)"
purpose: "Independent QA review of eval-loop body-diet refactor"
lifecycle: snapshot
use_when: "Tracing what was caught/missed in the eval-loop refactor session"
upstream: "eval-loop refactor commits"
---
```

**Pipeline artifact** (`write-copy`):

```yaml
---
skill: write-copy
version: 1
date: 2026-05-16
status: done
stack: marketing
review_surface: md
id: conquis-desktop-hero-v3
type: pipeline
keywords: [copy, hero, conquis]
summary: "Hero copy variants for conquis-desktop landing v3"
purpose: "Three hero-section copy variants scored against rubric"
lifecycle: pipeline
use_when: "Reviewing or selecting between Conquis desktop hero copy candidates"
upstream: "brand/BRAND.md, brief-landing-page output"
downstream: "humanmaxxing (terminal pass)"
---
```

**Reviewable canonical artifact** (`create-brand`, HTML preview surface):

```yaml
---
skill: create-brand
version: 1
date: 2026-05-26
status: done
stack: marketing
skills_involved: [research-icp, create-brand]
id: forsvn-brand-identity
type: canonical
keywords: [brand, identity, forsvn]
summary: "FORSVN brand identity — Pure Void palette, Bricolage + Be Vietnam Pro typography"
purpose: "Canonical brand identity record for downstream marketing + design work"
lifecycle: canonical
use_when: "Grounding any voice, visual, or token decision for FORSVN"
do_not_use_when: "The audience or positioning has materially changed since this was produced"
upstream: "research/icp-research.md, operator interview"
downstream: "write-copy, write-ad, write-social, brief-landing-page"
decision_state: pending
review_surface: html
review_tool: inline
reviewed_at:
reviewer:
---
```

While `decision_state: pending`, a co-located HTML preview lives at the same
flat path with extension `.html` — see [[reviewable-artifact-contract]] for the
review-surface lifecycle.

---

## Contract stability rule (Gate 4)

**The frontmatter shape is a contract.** Changing field names, removing required fields, or altering the lifecycle enum breaks downstream consumers:

- `manifest-sync.ts` indexing fails-fast
- Eval skills can't locate their inputs (e.g., `evaluate-landing-page` reads `brief-landing-page` frontmatter to validate version)
- `artifact-index.md` regeneration loses selection hints
- Historical artifact replay (eval replay during refactors) breaks

When the schema needs to change:
1. Change this template first
2. Open a coordinated change across all producing skills + manifest-sync.ts + eval consumers
3. Bump `version:` in every artifact written by changed producers
4. Run a full-stack regression sweep via the harness before merging

Do not change one skill's frontmatter without ripple coordination.

### v2 schema (2026-05-26)

The v2 schema renamed `review_state` → `decision_state` (with enum values
`rejected` → `denied` and `changes_requested` → `suggested`) and added the
mandatory `stack` + `review_surface` fields plus the optional `skills_involved`
list. Legacy artifacts that still carry `review_state` remain tolerated by
`manifest-sync` (one-line warning per artifact, normalized to the new field
name) for back-compat with external/older projects; `validate-artifacts
--strict` rejects `review_state` on any new artifact (rename it to
`decision_state`). The retired `stack: mkt` value is likewise normalized to
`marketing` by the indexer but rejected by `--strict`.

---

## How skills cite this ref

**In SKILL.md "Artifact Requirements" section:**

```
[See `references/_shared/artifact-contract-template.md` for the full frontmatter schema.]
Below is this skill's specific instance.
```

Then show only the fields that are skill-specific (typically `summary` template, `lifecycle` value, `upstream` / `downstream` values, custom fields). Don't restate the required four (`skill`, `version`, `date`, `status`) — those are universal.

**In agent prompts that produce artifacts:**

```
Write frontmatter per `references/_shared/artifact-contract-template.md`. This artifact is
lifecycle: <lifecycle>; required selection fields: summary, purpose, use_when.
```

---

## Anti-patterns

1. **Missing `status:`.** Every artifact must declare status. Defaulting to `done` silently breaks the Completion Status Protocol — operator can't tell ship-ready from partial output.
2. **Bare `lifecycle:` without selection fields.** If `lifecycle ≠ ephemeral` and `summary` / `purpose` / `use_when` are missing, `artifact-index.md` regen produces a "no description" row. Future agents can't choose this artifact intelligently.
3. **Mixed status values.** Don't invent new status values (`partial`, `wip`, `review`). The enum is fixed; use `done_with_concerns` for partials.
4. **Stale `version:`.** Bump version when re-writing an artifact in place (rare — usually create a new dated snapshot instead). Bumping signals "schema changed, re-validate" to downstream.
5. **Skipping `provenance:` on extracted content.** Gate 7 requires it. Without provenance, the chain-hardening audit can't reconstruct lineage when source material moves.
6. **`date:` set to artifact-edit time, not artifact-create time.** Date is the original snapshot date; if you're editing in place that's a sign you should be creating a new dated snapshot.

---
